jurisdiction: GHMC (Greater Hyderabad Municipal Corporation) / HMDA core urban area, Hyderabad
regime: Telangana Building Rules — G.O.Ms.No.168 MA&UD (07-04-2012) as amended; TG-bPASS (TS-bPASS Act 2020) processing

instantApproval.note: TG-bPASS instant registration track: individual residential buildings on plots up to 75 sq yd (about 62.71 m2) with height up to 7 m (G+1) require no formal permission - Re.1 token registration on self-declaration. Plots above 75 sq yd and up to 500 m2 with height up to 10 m get instant APPROVAL on self-certification; larger proposals go through single-window scrutiny (about 21 days). This pack models only the 75 sq yd / 7 m instant tier.

source.ref: TS-bPASS Act 2020 / TG-bPASS portal guidance (tsbpass.telangana.gov.in); docs/region-research.md - TG section

setbacks.note: Identical to the fixtures/code_rules.json TG band (<=100 m2), kept for engine compatibility. G.O.Ms.No.168 effectively waives side/rear setbacks for plots below ~50 m2 and for registered plots up to 75 sq yd (G+1); that relief is NOT modeled here - verify before relying on sub-50 m2 relaxations.

source.ref: G.O.Ms.No.168 MA&UD (2012) residential setback table; docs/region-research.md - TG setback note

setbacks.note: 101-200 m2 relaxed band per docs/region-research.md: front 1.5 m, sides and rear 1.0 m (road under 12 m).

source.ref: G.O.Ms.No.168 MA&UD (2012) residential setback table; docs/region-research.md - TG setback note

setbacks.note: 201-300 m2 band on a road under 12 m: front 2.0 m, sides and rear 1.5 m. The front setback increases with abutting road width (2 m up to ~12 m road, rising to 3 m+ for wider roads); road-width-scaled fronts are NOT modeled, to stay consistent with fixtures/code_rules.json.

source.ref: G.O.Ms.No.168 MA&UD (2012) residential setback table; docs/region-research.md - TG setback note
fixtures/code_rules.json TG setbacks (calibrated engine baseline); docs/region-research.md - TG section

setbacks.note: Baseline-engine extrapolation for large plots. G.O.Ms.No.168 prescribes height-dependent all-round setbacks for larger/taller buildings which this flat band does not capture.

source.ref: fixtures/code_rules.json TG setbacks (calibrated engine baseline); docs/region-research.md - TG section
G.O.Ms.No.168 height-vs-road-width provisions; docs/region-research.md - TG caveats (10 m on a road under 9 m)
G.O.Ms.No.168 height-vs-road-width provisions; docs/region-research.md - TG caveats (15 m on a 9-12 m road)

heightByRoad.note: Non-high-rise envelope on roads of 12 m and wider. Buildings of about 18 m and above are high-rise under the TG rules (separate fire, road-width and scrutiny requirements) and are outside this pack's scope.

source.ref: G.O.Ms.No.168 high-rise threshold (~18 m) - author inference from the rules' structure

far.note: Telangana Building Rules 2012 control non-high-rise residential buildability through setbacks + height; the post-2012 regime removed FAR-based control for these plot classes, so there is no separate FAR cap. The legacy engine (fixtures/code_rules.json TG.FAR = 1.5) uses 1.5 only as a practitioner working default for advisory massing on a <12 m road; a resolver consuming this pack should treat FAR as not-separately-capped and may fall back to 1.5 for advisory estimates only.

source.ref: G.O.Ms.No.168 MA&UD (2012) - setback-based control regime; docs/region-research.md - TG confidence note

coverage.note: Practitioner working default, consistent with fixtures/code_rules.json TG.maxGroundCoveragePct. The 2012 rules do not print a single coverage percentage for individual houses - achievable coverage is the residual after mandated setbacks.

parking.note: One covered car space (about 2.5 x 5.0 m) plus two-wheeler space per dwelling as the working default, typically met via stilt/ground parking (stilt up to 2.4 m clear is exempt from height reckoning and FAR-style computation). Formal ECS-based norms apply mainly to group housing / apartment developments above roughly 300-500 m2.

source.ref: G.O.Ms.No.168 parking provisions (practitioner reading); docs/region-research.md - TG parking row

rwh.note: Rainwater-harvesting structure (percolation pit) required as a sanction condition for plots of 200 m2 and above - WALTA-heritage requirement continued in Telangana and enforced by GHMC.

cornerPlot.note: Corner plots treat the second road-abutting side as a front setback (apply the matched band's frontM on that flank in place of sideM). A junction splay / corner cut-off may additionally be demanded by the authority.

source.ref: G.O.Ms.No.168 - corner plot treatment (common sanctioning practice)

roomMinimums.note: NBC-derived room minimums come from fixtures/code_rules.json states.TG (habitable 9.5 m2 / 2.4 m, kitchen 5.0 m2, ceiling 2.75 m, ventilation ratio 0.1, stair 1.0 m).

docChecklist: Latest property tax / vacant land tax receipt
Approved layout copy or LRS (layout regularisation) proceedings, where applicable
NALA conversion certificate (if the land was agricultural)
Building plans signed by the owner and a licensed technical person (LTP)
Structural stability certificate / soil report, as applicable to the proposed height
Mortgage deed for 10% of built-up area in favour of the authority, where applicable
Rainwater-harvesting undertaking (plots of 200 m2 and above)
Situational NOCs where triggered: airport funnel (RGIA Shamshabad), fire services, lake/nala FTL buffer, defence/railway proximity
notes: Advisory defaults for early design only - NOT legal advice and NOT a substitute for a sanctioned plan. Verify every value against the live TG-bPASS engine and the current G.O.Ms.No.168 tables for the specific plot.

Mortgage clause: the owner mortgages 10% of the proposed built-up area to the sanctioning authority (released after occupancy certificate) as security against deviations; commonly applied to plots above 200 m2 under the self-certification regime. Exact statutory threshold and mechanics NEED VERIFICATION against G.O.Ms.No.168 and the TS-bPASS rules.
Hyderabad-specific overlays NOT modeled: G.O. 111 catchment restrictions (Osmansagar/Himayatsagar), lake/nala full-tank-level buffers, RGIA airport funnel height clearances, heritage precincts.

Consistency: all setback bands for plots <= 500 m2 restate fixtures/code_rules.json TG values verbatim. Known deviations recorded in band notes: sub-50 m2 no-setback relief and road-width-scaled front setbacks (docs/region-research.md) are acknowledged but not modeled.
